title: These were Boston’s most popular dog names in 2025
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Luna was the most popular dog name in Boston for 2025, appearing on 137 licenses.
Labrador retrievers were the most common breed registered in Boston, with 1,961 dogs in 2025.
Boston dog owners must register pets over six months old by March 31; a $50 fine applies for non-compliance.

title: Another wintry mix? Here’s what meteorologists are saying about the timing and impacts.
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A wintry mix of rain, snow, and sleet is expected in southern New England Thursday night into Friday morning.
The National Weather Service warns of icy conditions impacting Thursday PM and Friday AM commutes, especially in higher elevations.
Northern Massachusetts may see a few inches of snow, while southeastern areas expect up to an inch of rain.

title: One of the best brewery tours in America is in New England, according to USA Today readers
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Allagash Brewing Co. in Portland, Maine, ranked No. 6 among the best brewery tours by USA Today readers.
Schilling Beer Co. in Littleton, NH, and Hill Farmstead Brewery in Greensboro Bend, VT, also received recognition.
Tröegs Independent Brewing in Hershey, PA, was voted the No. 1 brewery tour by USA Today readers.

title: Foul play suspected after human remains found in Shirley, police say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Human remains found in Shirley near the Maritime Veterans Memorial Bridge; foul play is suspected, officials confirm.
Discovery made by a group of young people Wednesday evening; investigation led by Middlesex DA and Shirley Police Chief.
State Police and dive team are assisting with investigation; increased police presence near Veterans Memorial Bridge expected.

title: Boston community grapples with fear and protest after U.S.-Israeli strike in Iran
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston protests erupted after U.S.-Israeli strike killed Iran's Supreme Leader, sparking international conflict.
Local Iranian-American residents express fear and anxiety for loved ones in Iran amid escalating tensions.
Mass. politicians and activists criticize the strike as illegal, urging legislative action against unauthorized warfare.

title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (March 5, 2026)
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: LPM Restaurant will open its first Northeast location in Back Bay, Boston, in the summer of 2026.
Cafe Noodo, located near TD Garden, offers hand-pulled noodles and customizable boba tea.
Call Me Honey opens in Cambridge, transforming from Curio Coffee with liège waffles and specialty coffee.

title: 3 reasons for optimism and 3 causes for concern as the Red Sox approach a new season
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Red Sox rely on promising young talent like Roman Anthony, who hit .292 with 8 home runs last season.
Veteran pitcher Garrett Crochet aims for a Cy Young as Red Sox bolster rotation with new additions.
Team faces potential injuries and chemistry issues during World Baseball Classic participation.

title: Boston police seeking suspect in armed robbery
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police are seeking a suspect in a Jan. 22 armed robbery in Dorchester.
The robbery occurred at 4:30 p.m. on Adams and Whitten Streets, involving multiple victims held at gunpoint.
Police urge anyone with information to contact detectives at 617-343-4335 or provide anonymous tips via CrimeStoppers.

title: No license yet for World Cup games as Foxborough asks for public safety funding up front
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Foxborough officials may withhold World Cup license due to concerns over $7.8 million required for public safety.
Boston FIFA World Cup 2026 host committee pledges to cover costs, but won't pay upfront, proposing payment within two days post-event.
Federal grant for security funding is pending; Massachusetts allocated over $46 million of $625 million total fund.

title: Wu to host first-ever ‘office hours’ for residents to speak with her directly
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu to host Boston's first 'office hours' event for residents at Bruce C. Bolling Building, Roxbury.
Event includes over a dozen city department staff to address questions and offer services like voting registration.
Wu announced the 'office hours' concept during her inaugural address to improve direct communication with residents.
